steps.title: Identify the critical constraint
steps.content: The passage explains the discrepancy by noting that historians of capitalism depend on “archival records,” which “do not exist for much of precolonial Africa.” Yet Alfagali has demonstrated that precolonial African capitalism did exist. This poses a logical puzzle: if the historians' standard source of evidence is unavailable, Alfagali must have found an alternative.

steps.title: Jump to the answer
steps.content: Alfagali's ability to demonstrate precolonial capitalism without the archival records that historians rely on directly implies she used different types of evidence—which is exactly what choice A states. B, C, and D either contradict the passage (B), address issues never mentioned (C on definitions, D on benefits), or lack support. That matches choice A — A is correct.
